features.name: Apple container run-plan generation
features.defaultDataFlow: Local command construction only. The plan prints paths, mount targets, network mode, and environment variable names but not secret values.

externalAi.fallback: Use local unit tests and `runhaven plan` review.

features.sensitivityNote: Mount, user, network, and env handling affect host-file and credential exposure.

features.name: Explicit environment passthrough
features.defaultDataFlow: Only variable names are accepted with `--env NAME`; inline values are rejected.
features.sensitivityNote: Environment variables may reference provider credentials even when values are not printed.

features.name: SSH agent forwarding
features.defaultDataFlow: Apple `container --ssh` forwards the agent socket without mounting raw SSH keys.

externalAi.fallback: Use local plan review and runtime smoke checks.

features.sensitivityNote: Forwarded SSH agent access can authorize private Git operations.

features.name: Bundled agent image builds
features.defaultDataFlow: Image builds fetch exact-pinned OS, npm, and binary artifacts from reviewed sources.

externalAi.fallback: Use registry APIs, lockfiles, checksums, and local build logs.

features.sensitivityNote: Image builds are supply-chain sensitive and require pin-policy verification.

features.name: Provider egress allowlisting and policy logs
features.defaultDataFlow: Provider mode routes allowed CONNECT requests through a host-side proxy on a managed internal network. Policy logs record sanitized host, port, decision, reason, profile, workspace, and run id metadata without request bodies or token values.

externalAi.fallback: Use `--network internal` for local-only commands or inspect decisions with `runhaven why host` and `runhaven egress log`.

features.sensitivityNote: Provider host choices can expose intended services and can widen egress if reviewed too broadly.

features.name: Codex API-key broker prototype
features.defaultDataFlow: The opt-in Codex broker reads the named host API-key environment variable, forwards allowed Responses API create requests, and logs sanitized method, path, allow/deny reason, upstream status, workspace, profile, and run id metadata. It does not log raw key values or request bodies.

externalAi.fallback: Skip broker smokes without a disposable key and use static `runhaven auth status` or `runhaven auth explain AGENT` diagnostics.

features.sensitivityNote: Broker expansion beyond Codex requires provider-specific design and evidence before credentials are handled.

features.name: Secret-free run, active-run, worktree, and git metadata
features.defaultDataFlow: Run records and active markers store run id, profile, workspace, network mode, state volume, status, cleanup summaries, and capped git metadata. They omit command lines, prompts, file contents, diffs, environment values, request bodies, and token values.

externalAi.fallback: Use local run-history, active-run, worktree, and state tests plus manual CLI smokes.

features.sensitivityNote: Workspace paths, changed-file names, and git state can reveal private project structure even when contents are omitted.
